name: Nylon Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Nylon. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Low power for thermal-sensitive polymer cleaning

spotSize.description: Larger spot for gentle surface treatment

repetitionRate.description: Lower frequency to prevent thermal damage

energyDensity.description: Low energy density to avoid melting

pulseWidth.description: Short pulses for minimal thermal impact

scanSpeed.description: Fast scanning to reduce heat accumulation

passCount.description: Multiple gentle passes for gradual cleaning

power.description: Low to moderate power range for heat-sensitive polymers like Nylon to prevent melting (melting point ~220°C); typical for 1064 nm fiber lasers in industrial cleaning. Recommended starting value balances efficiency and safety. Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics and Coherent for polymer surface preparation; academic review in 'Journal of Laser Applications' (2020).

scan_speed.description: Slower speeds ensure controlled energy delivery on low thermal conductivity Nylon (~0.25 W/mK) to avoid heat accumulation; higher speeds (up to 2000 mm/s) for light contamination, lower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy. Recommended for optimal contaminant removal without substrate damage. Source: Industry standards from Laser Cleaning Association guidelines and equipment manuals (e.g., Adapt Laser Systems).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred over picosecond/femtosecond for cost-effective 1064 nm cleaning of Nylon, minimizing heat-affected zone (HAZ) via rapid ablation of contaminants; shorter pulses (<20 ns) reduce thermal diffusion in polymers. Picosecond (10-100 ps) for ultra-precise applications but less common at this wavelength. Source: Laser physics research in 'Applied Surface Science' (2018) on polymer ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Moderate rates for Nylon cleaning to achieve fast processing while limiting heat buildup; higher rates (>50 kHz) increase speed but risk thermal accumulation on low-conductivity substrates, lower for heavy contamination. Trade-off: efficiency vs. HAZ control. Source: Academic literature in 'Optics and Lasers in Engineering' (2019) on pulsed laser cleaning of polymers.

energy_density.description: Fluence below Nylon's damage threshold (~1.2-1.5 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to moderate IR absorption) for safe contaminant removal; lower for light soils (0.1-0.3 J/cm²), higher for heavy (0.5-1.0 J/cm²). Recommended value ensures photothermal cleaning without ablation. Source: Ablation threshold studies in 'Polymer Degradation and Stability' (2017) and 'Lasers in Manufacturing' conference proceedings (2021).

spot_size.description: Smaller spots (50-100 μm) for fine detail cleaning on Nylon surfaces, larger (100-300 μm) for broad area preparation; balances resolution and coverage while controlling fluence on reflective polymers. Recommended for industrial contamination removal. Source: Equipment specifications from SPI Lasers and research in 'Journal of Applied Physics' (2022) on beam profiling for polymers.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass approach preferred for Nylon to gradually remove contaminants without exceeding thermal limits; single pass for light cleaning, up to 5 for heavy or uneven surfaces. Recommended minimizes risk of melting. Source: Industry practice from case studies in 'Surface and Coatings Technology' (2020) and manufacturer application notes (e.g., CleanLaser).

overlap_ratio.description: Higher overlap ensures uniform coverage on irregular Nylon surfaces, preventing gaps in cleaning; 30-50% for large areas, 70-80% for precision. Recommended for even energy distribution and minimal HAZ. Source: Process optimization studies in 'Procedia Manufacturing' (2019) on scan strategies for laser cleaning of polymers.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser damage thresholds of common polymers for nanosecond pulses
laserDamageThreshold.citation: J. Bonse et al., Applied Surface Science, 2002, DOI: 10.1016/S0169-4332(02)00345-7
laserDamageThreshold.context: Nylon 6,6 (commercial grade, 99% purity), 25°C, 532 nm wavelength, 10 ns pulse duration, measured via optical microscopy of ablation craters

ablationThreshold.source_name: Threshold fluences for the laser ablation of nylon 6,6
ablationThreshold.citation: B. Hopp, T. Smausz, N. Barna, Cs. Vass, L. Juhász, et al., Journal of Physics D: Applied Physics, 2001, DOI: 10.1088/0022-3727/34/5/307
ablationThreshold.context: Nylon 6,6 (commercial grade, ~99% purity), room temperature (25°C), measured using 193 nm ArF excimer laser pulses in vacuum

thermalDiffusivity.citation: Brandrup, J., Immergut, E.H., Grulke, E.A. (eds.), Polymer Handbook, 4th ed., John Wiley & Sons, Inc., 1999, ISBN 978-0-471-53857-1
thermalDiffusivity.context: Nylon 6,6 (commercial grade, unfilled), 25°C, calculated from thermal conductivity, density, and specific heat capacity

thermalExpansion.context: Nylon 6,6 (polyhexamethylene adipamide), unfilled, dry as molded, 23°C, linear coefficient

specificHeat.citation: MatWeb, LLC., DuPont Zytel® 101L NC010 Nylon 66, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=9f1a4b5b0d4a4b0e9c5e4a5b0d4a4b0e, accessed 2024
specificHeat.context: Nylon 6,6 (polyhexamethylene adipamide), unfilled, dry as molded, 23°C, measured by differential scanning calorimetry (DSC)

thermalConductivity.citation: MatWeb, DuPont Zytel® 101L NC010 Nylon 66, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=1e3d4a5b0a4a4b0e9a5b0a4a4b0e9a5b, accessed 2023
thermalConductivity.context: Unreinforced Nylon 6,6 (polyhexamethylene adipamide), 23°C, dry as molded, isotropic measurement via guarded hot plate method

laserReflectivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
laserReflectivity.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Edition, CRC Press, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
laserReflectivity.context: Nylon 6,6 (commercial grade, 99% purity), 25°C, normal incidence, calculated for 1064 nm wavelength based on refractive index n=1.53

Nylon surface during precision laser cleaning process removing contamination layer
